# ramgate fake-ps database. TAB-separated columns:
#   pid <TAB> rss_kb <TAB> uid <TAB> pgid <TAB> vsz_kb <TAB> pmem <TAB> lstart <TAB> comm
# lstart is the 5-token macOS `ps -o lstart=` string (weekday mon day HH:MM:SS year).
# The fake `ps` stub (fixtures/bin/ps) projects the requested -o columns out of this.
# Row roster (all uid 502 so they survive the same-user filter; protection is by comm):
#   5001 giant Chrome hog (biggest, NOT protected -> the size target)
#   5002 growable app     (fast grower when its prev RSS is seeded low)
#   5003 WindowServer     (protected essential)
#   5004 ram-guard        (the guard's OWN binary -> must never be a target)
#   5005 Ghostty          (user terminal -> protected)
#   5006 tmux             (terminal multiplexer -> protected)
#   5007 zsh              (login shell -> protected)
#   5008 tiny helper      (below MIN_RSS_MB -> ignored)
5001	4194304	502	5001	8388608	40.0	Thu Jul 2 16:58:11 2026	/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome Helper (Renderer)
5002	1048576	502	5002	2097152	10.0	Fri Jul 3 09:00:00 2026	/usr/bin/somebigapp
5003	2097152	502	5003	4194304	20.0	Mon Jun 1 08:15:30 2026	/System/Library/PrivateFrameworks/SkyLight.framework/Resources/WindowServer
5004	512000	502	5004	1024000	5.0	Thu Jul 2 10:00:00 2026	/Users/milan.santosi/projects/ramgate/bin/ram-guard
5005	900000	502	5005	1800000	8.0	Wed Jul 1 12:00:00 2026	/Applications/Ghostty.app/Contents/MacOS/Ghostty
5006	400000	502	5006	800000	3.0	Wed Jul 1 12:00:01 2026	/opt/homebrew/bin/tmux
5007	350000	502	5007	700000	2.0	Wed Jul 1 12:00:02 2026	/bin/zsh
5008	1024	502	5008	2048	0.1	Thu Jul 2 09:00:00 2026	/usr/bin/tiny
